methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceForest, a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rafał Przybyła, operates within a fragmented sector comprising 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 identifiable private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace. The company has raised capital via IPO and ESA funding.

Triangulation analysis encountered material constraints. Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters for stage alignment and valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints (need ≥3). Public-comp anchoring, the secondary method, could not be executed absent disclosed revenue figures—SpaceForest's top-line financials are not on file, precluding application of the sector's 53.2x P/S median observed across the six public comparables. Neither method generated quantitative output suitable for range estimation.

Without executable valuation triangulation, no specific range can be derived from available data. Key uncertainties include: revenue disclosure status and magnitude, which would unlock public-comp P/S anchoring; company-stage clarity relative to the peer universe (early-revenue vs. mature commercial operations); and capital intensity relative to SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and other reference points. Resolution of revenue visibility represents the primary path to analytical closure. Current data insufficiency precludes a defensible valuation synthesis.
